# Grant — Player Profile

**Callsign:** Grant | **Tracker:** Eh_Grant | **Role:** IGL / Flex | **Email:** grantgreenfelder@gmail.com

---

## Identity

**Strengths:** The team's IGL and best gun. Grant leads with reads, wins the isolated duels nobody else takes, and holds composure when the round is on the line.

**Tendencies:** Plays best when in full control of his own actions. Doesn't need frag assistance. Comfortable in isolated, high-pressure moments. Highest clutch win% on the team — composure is a consistent trait, not a season fluke.

**Role fit:** IGL / Flex

**Solo vs. Group:** #1 priority for solo roles in any formation.

---

## Career Coaching Notes

- **Best player on the team** by K/D and clutch metrics across tracked seasons. No structural weaknesses.
- **Chalet** is a recurring team-wide problem — not Grant-specific. Don't assign individual blame.
- Low HS% relative to teammates (mid-30s%) is consistent with his calm, cerebral aim style — not a weakness to correct.
- IGL role is appropriate and earns the stack its best outcomes. Don't pull him into rigid formed roles that limit his reads.

---

# Grant — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-04-03 (145M)_

---

## Your Role

IGL and primary fragger. You call the exec, you win the solo duel when it matters. The team plays around your reads — your job is to make those reads and give yourself the best possible op to execute them with. At 145M the Y11S1 picture is clear enough to draw some real conclusions from.

---

## What's Working

**DEF — This side is locked in.**
Your top four DEF ops are all producing:
- Mute — 166r | 53.6% | 1.23 ✅ (primary anchor, consistent)
- Kapkan — 60r | 56.7% | 1.90 ✅ (elite K/D, trap setups are working)
- Thorn — 53r | 60.4% | 1.91 ⭐ (top-tier WR and K/D)
- Castle — 48r | 64.6% | 1.39 ⭐ (strongest WR in your DEF pool)

That's 327 rounds across four ops all at 53%+ WR. The DEF formula is solid — don't overthink it.

**ATK — Thatcher is still the right primary.**
71r / 45.1% / 1.27 K/D. Thatcher's value is often team-enabling more than personal WR — breaches that convert for your teammates may not always show in your personal numbers directly. Hibana (41r / 46.3% / 1.28) is your next-highest-volume op and performing similarly. Both are reasonable foundations to keep building on.

**Maps — Three clear team strengths.**
Border (19M / 63.2%), Clubhouse (12M / 66.7%), Chalet (10M / 60.0%). All above 60% WR with 10+ matches. Villa (13M / 53.8%) is a solid next tier. These are worth protecting in the ban phase when you have a say.

---

## Areas to Watch

**ATK: Sledge and Lion are carrying a lot of rounds at lower WR.**
- Sledge — 52r | 36.5% | 1.05 ⚠️
- Lion — 41r | 36.6% | 0.82 ⚠️

These are your 2nd and 3rd most-played ATK ops, and both are sitting well below 40% WR. That could be site difficulty, matchup variance, or just a rough stretch — but at 40–50 rounds each, it's worth asking whether these are the right default picks for your playstyle. Glaz (10r / 70.0% ⭐) and Ash (8r / 62.5% ⭐) are both producing at the top of your pool in smaller samples — worth seeing if they can earn more rounds.

**ATK: A few ops may be worth pulling back on.**
- Gridlock — 21r | 23.8% ⚠️
- Flores — 22r | 31.8% ⚠️
- Deimos — 12r | 33.3% ⚠️

These three have built up 55 rounds combined with WR below 34%. It may be that they're being played in tough spots or experimental rounds — but if they're going in as serious picks, the results suggest they might not be the best fit for your style right now. Consider giving those rounds to Amaru or Hibana and see if the return is better.

**DEF: Bandit is trending poorly.**
17r / 29.4% / 0.47 ⚠️. With Mute already covering gadget denial at high volume and strong WR, Bandit may just not be adding much in the spots you're using it. Worth considering whether those Bandit rounds would be better spent elsewhere in your pool.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Think about tightening the ATK pool.**
Sledge and Lion together are 93 rounds at 36–37% WR. Whether that's a matchup issue or a comfort pick that isn't converting, it's worth experimenting with giving more of those rounds to Hibana and Amaru and tracking whether the result improves. You don't have to commit — just try the shift and see.

**2. Castle deserves deliberate placement on Clubhouse and Chalet.**
Castle DEF is your highest-WR op at 64.6% over 48 rounds. On maps where Castle walls create meaningful chokepoints — Clubhouse, Chalet, Consulate — it might be worth making Castle your first call rather than defaulting to Mute everywhere. Mute stays primary on most other maps. This is less about changing the formula and more about being intentional on which maps Castle is the right anchor.

**3. Coastline is worth a conversation.**
15 matches at 40.0% WR — below even. Your DEF side (45.0%) is where most of the loss is coming from. If the team doesn't have a clear defensive setup for the sites that come up on Coastline, it might be worth flagging it for the ban discussion rather than hoping for better results.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

# Peej — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-04-03 (81M)_

---

## Your Role

Entry fragger. You go in first, you create the opening. On DEF, Caveira is your anchor and it's one of the strongest individual op performances on the main stack right now. The role is confirmed and the results are showing it. The main thing to explore this season is whether Brava should be leading your ATK picks over Ash, given where the numbers are trending.

---

## What's Working

**DEF — Caveira is elite. This side is your clear strength.**
- Caveira — 90r | 64.4% | 1.77 ⭐ (dominant — highest WR in your DEF pool at primary volume)
- Thorn — 18r | 72.2% | 2.55 ⭐ (outstanding in secondary rounds)
- Valkyrie — 43r | 58.1% | 1.40 ✅ (reliable and consistent)

Caveira at 90 rounds and 64.4% WR is one of the strongest individual operator performances on the roster this season. Keep building. Thorn is producing exceptional numbers at 18 rounds — worth giving it more room.

**ATK — Brava is your most efficient pick right now.**
- Brava — 33r | 54.5% | 1.55 ✅
- Kali — 9r | 66.7% | 1.20 ⭐ (small sample — directional only, but encouraging)

Brava is winning rounds and posting a strong K/D. The toolkit (Kludge Virus hacking enemy gadgets) complements aggressive entry well. It's performing above the rest of your ATK pool by a meaningful margin.

**Maps — Villa and Border are your two dominant maps.**
Villa (6M / 83.3%), Border (9M / 77.8%), Bank (9M / 66.7%), Clubhouse (8M / 62.5%). Four maps above 60% WR with real samples. Villa especially — 83.3% over 6 matches is exceptional. Worth protecting these in the ban discussion.

---

## Areas to Watch

**ATK: Ash is your primary op at 65 rounds, but WR is sitting at 38.5%.**
Ash — 65r | 38.5% | 1.11 ⚠️

Ash is your career identity op and 1,200+ all-time rounds don't lie — you know how to play it. But this season at 65 rounds it's trending below 40% WR, while Brava (33r / 54.5%) is sitting 16 points higher. That gap could mean a few things — Ash might be going in on harder sites, or it might be getting played in spots where Brava's gadget disruption would convert more rounds, or it could be a coordination timing issue on entry. It's worth exploring whether shifting the default toward Brava-first this season changes the result. You don't have to drop Ash — just try leading with Brava on rounds where the two are interchangeable and see if the WR gap holds or closes.

**ATK: Twitch (55r / 43.6%) is also running below 45% WR at high volume.**
Twitch has a clear support role — disable cameras before entry — but 43.6% over 55 rounds suggests the team may not always be capitalizing fully on what Twitch opens up. If you're disabling cameras but the execute isn't adapting to the cleared intel, the op may not be adding as much value as it could. Worth checking in with the group about whether the Twitch opener is actually being used.

**DEF: Maestro is a question of how it's being played.**
Maestro — 46r | 47.8% / 0.81 K/D. Maestro's strength is post-plant denial through the Evil Eye. # Hound — Player Profile

**Callsign:** Hound | **Tracker:** Eh_Hound | **Role:** Support | **Email:** shadowhound6193@gmail.com

---

## Identity

**Strengths:** The backbone of the stack's coordinated pushes. Hound wins rounds through utility, callouts, and enabling the team's fraggers — one of the most consistently active players on the roster.

**Tendencies:** Group player. Passive support. Strong with utility operators. Rarely out of position — disciplined. Sub-1.0 K/D is a career pattern but team win rates stay positive because of his enabling role.

**Role fit:** Support Anchor

**Solo vs. Group:** Avoid solo roles. Weakest gun on the team — solo entry puts him in duels he is not built to win. Keep him in a group where utility is the contribution.

---

## Career Coaching Notes

- **Never assign solo entry.** This is a standing instruction across all seasons — profile confirmed by data.
- **DEF is where he contributes most individual value.** Attack is enabling work; defense is where individual efficiency shows.
- **Castle as primary DEF anchor** is a recurring pattern across seasons — worth investigating whether this is intentional coordination strategy or just comfort. If intentional, lean into barricade callout protocols.
- **Frost** consistently outperforms Castle on individual metrics — worth asking whether Frost should lead in volume.
- Attack K/Ds are low by design — he must always be grouped with a fragger who does the killing.
- **ATK role reminder:** Hound provides the breach/gadget clearance. Someone else gets the kills. This is working — don't break it.

mainStack.coachingContent:
# Hound — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-04-03 (91M)_

---

## Your Role

Team support and group anchor. On ATK, your value is in the group execute — enabling the push, not winning solo duels. On DEF, you hold site with utility and coordination. At 91M, the Y11S1 picture is showing a pattern on ATK worth paying attention to: most of the high-volume ops are trending below 40% WR, while some of the lower-volume picks are producing noticeably better.

---

## What's Working

**DEF — Azami and Frost are producing your best results.**
- Azami — 29r | 62.1% | 1.15 ⭐ (best WR in your DEF pool with meaningful volume)
- Frost — 26r | 57.7% | 1.06 ✅ (outperforms Castle on WR and K/D)
- Bandit — 39r | 53.8% ✅ (reliable secondary)
- Maestro — 12r | 66.7% | 1.25 ⭐ (small sample, but a strong positive signal)

Your DEF ceiling is genuinely high when you're on these ops. They're winning rounds at a rate that Castle and Mute aren't matching right now.

**ATK — Rauora and Gridlock are your best-performing picks.**
- Rauora — 15r | 53.3% | 0.73 ✅
- Gridlock — 11r | 72.7% | 4.00 ⭐ (very small sample — treat as directional only)
- Buck — 7r | 57.1% | 1.40 (small but positive)

These three fit your support role well — area denial, flank coverage, group enable. They're also outperforming everything above them in your op list by rounds. That's worth noticing.

**Maps — Border, Clubhouse, and Bank are your bright spots.**
Border (14M / 57.1%), Clubhouse (8M / 62.5%), Bank (6M / 66.7%), Coastline (11M / 54.5%). These four are your best options right now for ban-phase protection.

---

## Areas to Watch

**ATK: Most of the high-volume ops are trending below 40% WR.**

| Op | Rounds | WR | Note |
|----|--------|----|------|
| Thermite | 40r | 35.0% | ⚠️ |
| Osa | 38r | 36.8% | ⚠️ |
| Grim | 30r | 43.3% | — |
| Thatcher | 29r | 41.4% | ⚠️ |
| Nomad | 28r | 32.1% | ⚠️ |
| Fuze | 23r | 39.1% | ⚠️ |

Five of the top six by round count are flagged. That could mean a lot of things — tough sites, matchup variance, a team coordination pattern on certain ops — so it's worth thinking about rather than assuming it's op selection alone. That said, Rauora (53.3%) and Gridlock (72.7%) are both outperforming this whole group in smaller samples, and both fit your support role just as well. It's at least worth trying to shift some volume toward them and seeing if the round results feel different.

**DEF: Castle is leading your DEF by rounds but sits below your other options on WR and K/D.**
Castle — 53r | 49.1% | 0.79. Azami (62.1%) and Frost (57.7%) are both outperforming it by a meaningful margin, and they have enough rounds now to say that's a real pattern. If Castle is your default pick on most sites out of habit, it might be worth asking whether Azami or Frost would be a better fit before the round starts — especially on sites where you don't specifically need the barricade utility that Castle brings.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Try giving Rauora more ATK volume.**
It's at 15 rounds and 53.3% WR — the best-performing meaningful ATK pick you have. It fits your support identity naturally (area denial, flank coverage) and the early results are encouraging. See if you can get it up to 30+ rounds and let a larger sample tell the story.

**2. Question Osa before each round.**
38 rounds at 36.8% is a pattern worth examining. That said — if Osa is being used for specific utility (shield for plant cover, corner peek control) and the team is capitalizing on it, the WR might not tell the full story. The question to ask yourself before going Osa: does the team have a plan to use what Osa provides? If not, Rauora or Grim might give better return.

**3. DEF: Try Azami or Frost as your default before defaulting to Castle.**
Castle has a clear role — barricade utility on specific sites — but if it's becoming the automatic pick on most rounds, it might be worth swapping the default. Try going Azami or Frost first and using Castle deliberately on the sites where the walls matter. The WR difference (62% vs. 49%) is meaningful enough to experiment.

**4. Stay grouped — this is still the standing instruction.**
Your gun is not the team's fragger. Your value comes from the group execute — when you're running with Smigs and Grant, the team's coordination is at its strongest. If a push opportunity comes up and it's a solo run, hold it and wait for the group to be ready.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

It's at 15 rounds and 53.3% WR — the best-performing meaningful ATK pick you have. It fits your support identity naturally (area denial, flank coverage) and the early results are encouraging. See if you can get it up to 30+ rounds and let a larger sample tell the story.

**2. Question Osa before each round.**
38 rounds at 36.8% is a pattern worth examining. That said — if Osa is being used for specific utility (shield for plant cover, corner peek control) and the team is capitalizing on it, the WR might not tell the full story. The question to ask yourself before going Osa: does the team have a plan to use what Osa provides? If not, Rauora or Grim might give better return.

**3. DEF: Try Azami or Frost as your default before defaulting to Castle.**
Castle has a clear role — barricade utility on specific sites — but if it's becoming the automatic pick on most rounds, it might be worth swapping the default. Try going Azami or Frost first and using Castle deliberately on the sites where the walls matter. The WR difference (62% vs. 49%) is meaningful enough to experiment.

**4. Stay grouped — this is still the standing instruction.**
Your gun is not the team's fragger. Your value comes from the group execute — when you're running with Smigs and Grant, the team's coordination is at its strongest. If a push opportunity comes up and it's a solo run, hold it and wait for the group to be ready.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

mainStack.profileContent:
# Smigs — Player Profile

**Callsign:** Smigs | **Tracker:** Eh_tooten | **Role:** Support

⚠️ **Callsign note:** Some old script files and earlier KB docs swap Smigs/Sarge. Authoritative mapping: **Smigs = Eh_tooten**, Sarge = Eh_SiegeyBodeez. Trust tracker usernames over callsign labels in any document with ambiguity.

---

## Identity

**Strengths:** The most reliable support player on the roster. Smigs brings a team-first mentality to every round — utility clearing, hard breach, and group entry without needing the frag lead.

**Tendencies:** Does what the team needs without hesitation. Contributes to wins at a high rate without requiring the frag lead. Consistently high win rates relative to K/D — a reliable indicator of support-first play style.

**Role fit:** Utility / Hard Breach

**Solo vs. Group:** Avoid solo entry pushes. Keep Smigs in a group.

---

## Career Coaching Notes

- **Win rate is the signal, not K/D.** His support-first mentality produces wins consistently. Don't coach him toward fragging — it'll break what works.
- **Capitão** was a Y10S4 standout — 60.9% WR in 23r, the highest ATK win rate in his pool that season. Not yet in his all-time ATK top 5 (Thatcher/Thermite/Nomad/Zofia/Ace), so the sample is still growing. Worth giving more volume in Y11S1 to confirm if this is a trend.
- **Skyscraper** was a personal weakness in Y10S4 (40.0% WR, 0.52 K/D in 5 matches) but all-time he sits at 50.0% on Skyscraper — so this is season-specific, not a career pattern. Do not treat as a permanent avoidance map.
- **Stack play is where he adds the most value.** His WR in stack vs. solo queue is meaningfully different — the more he plays with the team, the better the outcomes.
- Zone-control DEF anchor (Kaid/Aruni/Castle) is a stable identity across seasons.

---

mainStack.coachingContent:
# Smigs — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-04-03 (39M)_

---

## Your Role

Utility anchor and group execute player. On ATK, Thermite is your anchor and it's working well. On DEF, your pool is stacked — Bandit, Thorn, Mute, and Kaid are all producing strong numbers. The main thing to think about this season is DEF op selection: Aruni is your primary by rounds, but the ops below it in the rotation are outperforming it by a significant margin. Worth exploring whether that's a site-fit issue or a habit issue.

---

## What's Working

**ATK — Thermite is doing its job.**
Thermite — 65r | 47.7% | 1.34. Solid primary at close to 48% WR. Nomad (12r / 66.7% ⭐) and Ace (11r / 54.5% ✅) are both producing well in secondary volume — worth letting those build naturally.

**DEF — The pool is genuinely deep.**
Look at what's winning rounds:
- Bandit — 15r | 73.3% ⭐ (your best DEF WR this season)
- Mute — 10r | 70.0% ⭐
- Castle — 8r | 62.5% ⭐
- Thorn — 15r | 60.0% ⭐
- Kaid — 24r | 58.3% ✅

Five ops at 58%+ WR. That's a strong DEF pool and reflects real depth. The question is just whether the round count is going to the right ops given those results.

**Maps — Clubhouse is a personal strength this season.**
Clubhouse (5M / 80.0%), Nighthaven Labs (5M / 60.0%), Oregon (3M / 66.7%). Samples are still small, but all positive. Clubhouse in particular has historically been tough for the team — the 80% is a meaningful shift worth keeping an eye on.

---

## Areas to Watch

**DEF: Aruni (30r / 46.7%) is your primary, but it's sitting below four other ops in your pool.**
Aruni is getting the most DEF rounds and posting 46.7% WR, while Bandit (73.3%), Mute (70.0%), Thorn (60.0%), and Kaid (58.3%) are all sitting well above it. That doesn't necessarily mean Aruni is the wrong pick — there may be specific sites or setups where the Prisma laser grid adds real value that the WR doesn't fully capture. But it's worth asking before each round whether Aruni is the right fit for that site, or whether Bandit, Mute, or Kaid would convert more often given what the site calls for.

**ATK: Hibana is trending poorly.**
Hibana — 13r | 15.4% ⚠️. That's a low WR at enough rounds to notice. It could be site difficulty, or hard breach going into tough spots, or just a rough run — but if Hibana is being queued as a regular second hard-breach option, it might be worth comparing it against Ace (11r / 54.5%) which is producing much better in similar volume. Thermite handles the primary breach role well — if a secondary breach op is needed, Ace looks like the stronger candidate right now.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Think about DEF op selection before each round.**
The numbers suggest that Aruni might be going in by default on rounds where Bandit or Mute would actually be a better fit. Before a DEF round, a quick mental check — does this site need the Prisma grid specifically, or is gadget denial (Bandit/Mute) or trap pressure (Thorn/Kaid) the better call? You don't need to drop Aruni, just be deliberate about when it's the right tool versus when something else would win the round more reliably.

**2. Kaid is worth more rounds.**
24 rounds at 58.3% WR is a strong, meaningful sample. Kaid's Rtila Electric Claw handles electrical surface denial and provides barricade in one gadget — on sites like Oregon, Villa, or Clubhouse where that combination adds real defensive value, it's a genuinely strong anchor option. Worth building its round count toward 40+ to get a clearer read.

**3. Thermite is confirmed — keep building on it.**
65 rounds at 47.7% WR is a solid primary. The supporting ATK picks to grow alongside it are Nomad (12r / 66.7%) and Ace (11r / 54.5%) — both small samples but both trending well. Let them build volume naturally rather than forcing it.

**4. Your win rate is still the right metric.**
Your Y11S1 RIS is 63.6 — highest on the team. That reflects consistent round contribution even in rounds where the frag count is low. A round where your gadgets create the condition for a teammate to win the 1v1 is a round you did your job. The formula works.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

# Sarge — Player Profile

**Callsign:** Sarge | **Tracker:** Eh_SiegeyBodeez | **Role:** Entry / Fragger

⚠️ **Callsign note:** Some old script files and earlier KB docs swap Smigs/Sarge. Authoritative mapping: **Sarge = Eh_SiegeyBodeez**, Smigs = Eh_tooten. Trust tracker usernames over callsign labels in any document with ambiguity.

---

## Identity

**Strengths:** A two-way player whose real identity lives on defense. Vigil is his career anchor, and on attack he's most effective leading the three-man group rather than working solo.

**Tendencies:** Coordination similar to Grant but less composed under pressure. Excels at getting early man advantage or map control. Comfortable leading a sub-group. **Defense is his dominant side** — this is a consistent career pattern, not just a season anomaly.

**Role fit:** Sub-IGL / Anchor

**Solo vs. Group:** Most valuable leading a 2–3 man group. Solo roles should go to Grant or Peej first (Slug B Team as of 2026-03-21).

---

## Career Coaching Notes

- **Role/op mismatch is the standing coaching priority.** Called "Entry/Fragger" but his Y10S4 ATK top 5 (Thermite/Buck/IQ/Sledge/Hibana) posted sub-1.0 K/D on every operator this season. Note: all-time, three of his top 5 ATK ops have hit 1.0+ K/D (Ace 1.15, Thatcher 1.02, Sledge 1.02) — so the issue is Y10S4 op selection, not solely mechanics. Either evolve toward actual entry ops (Ash, Zofia, Iana) or update the role label.
- **Defense is his real identity.** Vigil is the career DEF anchor — all-time #1 DEF op (716r / 57.8% / 1.45 K/D, Y8S1+). Thunderbird is a strong Y10S4 addition (115r this season) but does not appear in his all-time top 5 DEF ops (Vigil/Mute/Bandit/Doc/Rook) — treat it as emerging, not career-proven. In stack play, his DEF rounds are the priority.
- **Rank gap** (historically Platinum while the rest of the stack sits at Emerald) needs a direct conversation. Solo queue vs. stack performance should be investigated.
- In stack formation: assign Sarge to lead the 3-man group on attack while Grant/Peej take solo roles (Slug B Team as of 2026-03-21). His coordination capability makes him the best choice for sub-group IGL.
- **Outback, Border, Villa** are recurring strong maps — protect these in ban phase.
- **Chalet** is a consistent team-wide problem, not Sarge-specific.

mainStack.coachingContent:
# Sarge — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-04-03 (117M)_

---

## Your Role

Sub-IGL and roam threat. On DEF, Vigil roaming is your identity — your K/D on Vigil is the highest individual defensive K/D on the main stack. On ATK, you lead the 3-man group and have built a real Zofia identity over 163 rounds this season. The Zofia transition worked. The area worth digging into now is Jackal, which is carrying a lot of rounds at lower WR.

---

## What's Working

**DEF — Vigil and Azami are your 1–2.**
- Vigil — 105r | 56.2% | 1.39 ✅ (roaming identity, elite K/D)
- Azami — 62r | 59.7% | 1.24 ✅ (strong anchor complement)
- Melusi — 15r | 60.0% ⭐ (smaller sample, but a positive signal worth watching)

167 rounds across your top two ops at 57%+ WR. DEF is your dominant side. When you roam on Vigil, a simple heads-up to the anchors before the round ("going X flank, back in 90") helps the rest of the team play their anchor without guessing your position.

**ATK — Zofia identity is confirmed.**
163r / 43.6% / 1.20 K/D — a full season's worth of volume and K/D solidly above 1.0. That's a meaningful step up from Y10S4 where every ATK op was sub-1.0 K/D. The transition landed. Thatcher (16r / 56.2% ✅) and Iana (13r / 61.5% ⭐) are both producing well in secondary rounds — worth letting those grow.

**Maps — Four clear strengths.**
Clubhouse (11M / 72.7%), Coastline (9M / 66.7%), Border (14M / 64.3%), Chalet (10M / 60.0%). All above 60% WR with solid samples. These four are worth protecting in the ban phase when you have a say.

---

## Areas to Watch

**ATK: Jackal is carrying 62 rounds at 32.3% WR — worth a closer look.**
Jackal — 62r | 32.3% | 1.18 ⚠️

That's your second-most-played ATK op this season, and WR is sitting at 32.3%. There are a few possible explanations — it may be getting queued in tough matchups, or the drone-track intel isn't translating to round wins in team play, or it's just running in a rough stretch. But at 62 rounds the trend is consistent enough to be worth questioning. It might be worth pulling Jackal back and giving those rounds to Zofia or Thatcher for a stretch, then seeing if the overall ATK WR shifts. If the WR recovers, that's a useful signal about where those rounds are best spent.

**Maps — Oregon and Consulate are trending poorly at meaningful sample sizes.**
- Oregon — 14M | 28.6% (ATK 35.0%, DEF 42.9% — both sides struggling)
- Consulate — 11M | 27.3% (ATK side at 40.0% is where it's slipping)

These could be rough matchup clusters or specific site problems — hard to know from WR alone. But the sample sizes are real enough to flag. If the team is looking for ban targets and you're drawing Oregon or Consulate often, it's worth raising. Oregon in particular has both sides below 43%.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Experiment with pulling Jackal back.**
It's not a certainty that Jackal is the problem — but 62 rounds at 32.3% is enough of a pattern to test against. Try a session or two leaning on Zofia primary + Thatcher secondary and track whether the ATK results feel different. If the team's breach timing improves when Jackal isn't in the loop, that's a real answer. If results stay flat, it may be something else entirely.

**2. Oregon might be worth flagging in your ban discussions.**
With 14 matches at 28.6%, it's your lowest-WR map with a real sample. You know this map better than the stats do — if there are specific sites or setups that are genuinely tough for how you play, it's worth making the case to the group. Don't just absorb the map because nobody said anything.

**3. Vigil roam — communicate the plan.**
Your Vigil K/D is elite, but roaming value multiplies when the anchors know where you are. A quick call before round start about where you're going and when to expect contact helps the team play with you instead of reacting to you. It doesn't take long and the round flow gets cleaner.

**4. Protect your four map strengths in ban phase.**
Clubhouse (72.7%), Coastline (66.7%), Border (64.3%), Chalet (60.0%) are your best four this season. When the team is negotiating ban phase, make the case for keeping these. They replaced the older Outback/Villa priority that's now less relevant.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

# Slug — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-04-03 | 62M sample_

---

## Your Role

You're the second IGL, the B Team's most experienced solo-queue player, and the player most comfortable operating independently. You thrive flanking, watching rotations, and making self-directed reads. Your DEF identity has shifted this season — Valkyrie is now your primary anchor, with Solis as a strong second. You get a solo role in team formations because your individual decision-making has earned it.

---

## What's Working

**Operators — DEF**
Valkyrie (58r / 67.2% ⭐) is your clear DEF anchor in Y11S1 — the intel-and-wallbang identity suits your read-heavy style well. Solis (25r / 60.0% ⭐) is a strong second pick. Castle (10r / 60.0%) is a solid third. All three are above 60% WR with meaningful volume — this DEF core is locked in.

**Operators — ATK**
Zero (10r / 70.0% ⭐) and Capitao (9r / 66.7% ⭐) are your two best-performing ATK ops this season, both with sample sizes worth taking seriously. Thermite (20r / 55.0%) is also producing positively. The WR signal on Zero and Capitao is meaningful — these should be getting more rounds.

**Maps**
Oregon (10M / 50.0%) and Border (8M / 50.0%) are your steadiest maps with positive K/D on both — consistent results at meaningful sample sizes. Bank (3M / 66.7%) is a bright spot but still a small sample.

---

## Areas to Explore

**Operators — ATK**
Solid Snake (26r / 30.8% / 0.76 K/D) and Buck (23r / 21.7%) are your two highest-volume ATK picks, and both are trending well below 50%. It may be worth gradually shifting rounds toward Zero and Capitao — both are outperforming your primary picks by a wide margin. The stats don't always tell the full story of why a pick underperforms (matchup variance, site difficulty, coordination factors can all play a role), but the gap is wide enough that it's worth experimenting with the volume distribution.

**Operators — DEF**
Smoke (23r / 43.5%) was your career anchor and is now your third most-played DEF op, sitting below baseline. That may be a reflection of matchup context this season rather than a fundamental decline — Smoke's value is heavily dependent on post-plant scenarios and team coordination. Worth tracking to see if the WR recovers as the season progresses.

**Maps**
Nighthaven Labs (6M / 16.7%) is a map worth flagging in ban discussions. Lair (3M / 0.0%) is a clear avoid — both ATK (14.3%) and DEF (37.5%) WR are low there.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Consider giving Zero and Capitao more ATK volume.**
Both are outperforming Solid Snake and Buck significantly in Y11S1. It's worth experimenting with either as your primary ATK pick for a session or two and tracking whether the trend holds at higher volume.

**2. Valkyrie is your new DEF identity — build around it.**
The intel-wallbang model suits your read-heavy playstyle. Solis and Castle are strong secondaries. Your DEF core is in a good place — the main coaching lever right now is on the ATK side.

**3. Nighthaven and Lair are your maps to flag at ban time.**
You don't control the main stack ban phase, but when your lineup has input, those are the two maps worth pushing against. Oregon and Border are your steadiest ground — protect them.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

# Krafty — Player Profile

**Callsign:** Krafty | **Tracker:** Eh_KraftySmokes | **Team:** B Team

---

## Identity

**Strengths:** A developing player with strong mechanical aim and an upward rank trajectory. Lesion DEF anchor is his established identity, with ATK still taking shape.

**Tendencies:** Newest and least experienced player. Decisions and positioning are the primary gap — not aim. Plays passive utility-heavy DEF well (Lesion traps). ATK pool has been fragmented and inconsistent.

**Role fit:** DEF Anchor

**Solo vs. Group:** Benefits from group play — fewer decisions to make independently.

---

## Career Coaching Notes

- **High HS% + low K/D = positioning/decision problem, not aim.** The coaching priority is always "when NOT to peek" — not mechanics.

# Bob — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-04-03 | 56M sample_

---

## Your Role

You're the B Team's DEF anchor and the player with the most to gain from finding a stable ATK identity. Your DEF side has clear bright spots — Maestro, Kaid, Thorn, and Frost are all producing well. The ATK side is where the work is: all four primary ATK ops are currently trending below 40% WR, and finding one or two that hold up over time will directly improve your overall WR. This is a solvable problem.

---

## What's Working

**Operators — DEF**
Your DEF has real strengths — it's just not Kapkan leading the way anymore. Maestro (12r / 75.0% ⭐) and Kaid (9r / 66.7% ⭐) are your two best-performing DEF ops this season, with Thorn (29r / 55.2% ✅) providing reliable volume at above-baseline WR. Frost (5r / 80.0%) is a small sample but a strong early signal. These four are the ops worth leaning into on DEF. Kapkan (51r / 49.0%) is still getting the most DEF rounds but is sitting just below 50% — it's still a fine pick, but Maestro and Kaid are outperforming it significantly if the site allows.

**Maps**
Villa (7M / 57.1%) and Clubhouse (5M / 60.0%) are your two best maps this season — both with positive overall WR and strong DEF-side splits. These are your confidence maps right now.

**HS%**
Your HS% has recovered to 39.8% in Y11S1 — much closer to your career average. That's a positive mechanical sign after the Y10S4 dip to 24.8%.

---

## Areas to Explore

**Operators — ATK**
All four primary ATK picks are trending below 40%: Nomad (31r / 32.3%), Brava (29r / 37.9%), Thermite (26r / 38.5%), Thatcher (24r / 16.7%). The Thatcher WR in particular is worth a closer look — 16.7% over 24 rounds is a significant signal, though it may partly reflect matchup or breach timing factors rather than pure operator issues. Worth considering whether Thatcher rounds could go to Hibana (5r / 60.0%) or Amaru (9r / 44.4%) — both are outperforming with smaller samples.

**Operators — DEF**
Vigil (11r / 27.3% ⚠️) and Valkyrie (8r / 37.5% ⚠️) are both below baseline in meaningful volume. These may be worth pulling back in favor of more Maestro or Kaid rounds where the site allows it.

**Maps**
Oregon (4M / 25%) and Bank (4M / 25%) are struggling this season — notable because both were career strengths. The samples are still small enough that these could reflect variance rather than a permanent shift, but they're worth monitoring at the next pull.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Explore giving Maestro and Kaid more DEF rounds where possible.**
Both are significantly outperforming Kapkan in Y11S1. Kapkan is still a good pick — but if the site has strong camera angles or Kaid trap placements, it may be worth considering those ops first before defaulting to Kapkan. The WR gap is wide enough to be worth experimenting.

**2. Consider whether Thatcher rounds could go elsewhere.**
16.7% WR over 24 rounds is a steep number. It may reflect coordination factors (breach timing, team sequencing) rather than the op itself — but if the pattern holds, Hibana or another soft breacher may be worth trying as the primary breach pick for a session.

**3. Lean on Villa and Clubhouse.**
These are your two best maps this season. When your lineup has a say in ban discussions, protecting these is worth flagging.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

Both are significantly outperforming Kapkan in Y11S1. Kapkan is still a good pick — but if the site has strong camera angles or Kaid trap placements, it may be worth considering those ops first before defaulting to Kapkan. The WR gap is wide enough to be worth experimenting.

**2. Consider whether Thatcher rounds could go elsewhere.**
16.7% WR over 24 rounds is a steep number. It may reflect coordination factors (breach timing, team sequencing) rather than the op itself — but if the pattern holds, Hibana or another soft breacher may be worth trying as the primary breach pick for a session.

**3. Lean on Villa and Clubhouse.**
These are your two best maps this season. When your lineup has a say in ban discussions, protecting these is worth flagging.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

# Hunter — Player Profile

**Callsign:** Hunter | **Tracker:** Meh_Hunter | **Team:** B Team

_Added: 2026-03-14 | ALL_TIME + Y10S4 pulled: 2026-03-15 | Previous username: Eh_Burttis (Aug 2023)_

---

## Identity

**Strengths:** One of the deepest DEF pools in the group — Aruni, Caveira, Valkyrie, and Thorn all above 56% WR with substantial rounds. A reliable hard-anchor identity when active.

**Tendencies:** ATK side is Ash-dominant (612r) but consistently loses — 44.4% career WR on the #1 ATK op. The entire ATK pool is sub-50%. Hunter earns rounds on DEF, not ATK. Despite this, K/D stays near 1.0 all-time, which suggests individual combat is fine — round wins on ATK come from better coordination, not aim.

**Role fit:** DEF Specialist

**Solo vs. Group:** Career data (1,069 ranked) shows a real, experienced player. Best season was Deadly Omen (205 matches, 3,457 RP). Y10S4 was light (22M). Activity has dropped in recent seasons — B Team classification based on career metrics (0.98 K/D, deep DEF pool, meaningful map data).

---

## Career Coaching Notes

- **Career metrics are B Team level.** K/D 0.98, 1,069 ranked career, deep DEF op pool, 155 map matches tracked — this is a real player. Activity has been low recently; pull Y11S1 at 10+ matches like other B Team players.
- **DEF pool is exceptional.** Aruni/Caveira/Valkyrie/Thorn all above 56% WR with 99+ rounds — consistent across all four. Kaid (65.3%), Fenrir (64.3%), Warden (62.9%) are also solid secondary picks.
- **Mira (24r, 70.8% WR)** is the highest-WR op with 20+ rounds career-wide. Underplayed — worth leaning into.
- **ATK needs direction.** Drop Ying (61r, 31.1% WR) and Iana (33r, 30.3%) entirely. Gridlock (29r, 55.2%) is actually the best ATK WR in the pool — counterintuitive but worth noting. Ash stays as the primary but needs site-reading improvement.
- **Oregon (25%, 8M)** — hard avoid in any combined game. Chalet (60%, 10M) and Kanal (85.7%, 7M) are strong picks with Hunter in the group.

# Hunter — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-04-03 | 19M sample_
_⚠️ All notes below are directional only — 19M is not enough for high-confidence conclusions. Re-pull at 30M._

---

## Your Role

You're the newest member of the B Team and still early in building your Y11S1 data picture. At 19 matches, the numbers give directional signals but not definitive reads — the story can shift meaningfully over the next 10–15 games. What the data does show early: your DEF side looks like a genuine strength, and your ATK side is the area to develop. That's a reasonable starting point.

---

## What's Working

**Operators — DEF**
All three primary DEF ops are above 50% WR: Aruni (17r / 52.9% ✅), Castle (15r / 53.3% ✅), Thorn (9r / 55.6% ✅). That's a consistent early signal — DEF looks like your more comfortable side. The ops themselves suit a passive, trap-and-reinforce approach, which appears to be working. Valkyrie (5r / 20.0%) is the outlier on DEF — still a very small sample so not worth over-weighting, but one to keep an eye on.

**Tracker username note**
Your current tracker username is Eh_Burttis (reverted from Meh_Hunter in April 2026). Stats are pulled under Eh_Burttis — make sure any future pull uses the current username.

---

## Areas to Develop

**Operators — ATK**
Ash (18r / 50.0%) is the only ATK op breaking even. Striker (9r / 33.3%), Ace (8r / 37.5%), and Brava (7r / 28.6%) are all below 50% — but the samples are small enough that these readings are highly volatile. These are early signals rather than conclusions. The more useful question at this stage is: which one or two ATK ops do you want to commit to building volume on? Ash at 50% is the natural starting anchor.

**Overall WR**
36.8% overall WR is low, but with 19M the variance is high — a few rounds swinging differently can change the WR picture significantly. Not a cause for concern yet, but worth watching at the 30M re-pull.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Re-pull at 30M — that's the real baseline.**
At 19 matches, coaching notes are necessarily limited. The 30M re-pull will give a cleaner picture of your ATK op performance, overall WR trend, and first map data. Everything below will get updated at that point.

**2. Build on the DEF identity.**
Aruni, Castle, and Thorn are all producing above 50% early. That's the side to lean into while you develop your ATK consistency. The passive trap-and-reinforce model is a good foundation for where you're at.

**3. Keep Ash as your ATK anchor for now.**
It's the one ATK op with a positive signal (50%). Continue building volume there while experimenting with secondary picks. Don't spread rounds too thin across too many ATK ops — the fragmentation makes it harder to build pattern recognition on any single op.

---

_Detailed operator tables and full season data are in your Y11S1.md file. Map data will be added at 30+ match milestone._

**Strengths:** DEF anchor with strong hard-denial pool. Bandit (67.4% WR), Frost (65.1%), Azami (63.0%), Castle (61.2%), Kaid (55.4%), Kapkan (57.9%) — multiple DEF ops above 55% WR with 30+ rounds. Consistent hard-anchor identity.

**Tendencies:** Breach-utility on ATK (Buck/Thatcher/Thermite high-volume) with below-average results. The raw K/D (0.65) undersells DEF contributions — utility placements, traps, and anchor positioning generate round wins that don't show up in K/D. Zero (70r, 45.7% WR, 0.72 K/D) is the most efficient ATK op and a better choice than Buck/Thatcher.

**Role fit:** Hard-anchor DEF specialist. Mira (11r, 72.7% WR, 1.14 K/D) is a standout small-sample stat — could develop into a primary Mira main. Fenrir (6r, 1.50 K/D) also noteworthy.

**Solo vs. Group:** Insufficient recent data. 307 career ranked matches shows experience. Best season was High Calibre (Y8S4): 44M, 2,884 MMR, 0.72 K/D. Recent seasons near-absent (2M in Y10S4, 0M in Y11S1).

---

## Career Coaching Notes

- **Buck/Thatcher ATK is high-volume but losing** — Buck 148r at 37.2% WR, Thatcher 97r at 36.1%. These ops are losing rounds consistently. Zero (45.7%) and Thermite (51.2%) are better ATK options by WR.
- **DEF is the genuine strength.** Bandit leading at 67.4% WR (43r) is rare for a non-main-stack player. Multiple DEF ops consistently above 60% — strong anchor identity.
- **Mira is the hidden gem** — 72.7% WR, 1.14 K/D in 11r. Worth developing. If D-Man can reliably play Mira, that's a legitimate stack asset on DEF.
- **Fenrir (6r, 1.50 K/D)** — tiny sample but the highest K/D of any op with 5+ rounds. Possible fragger upside on DEF.
- First useful pull target: 10+ Y11S1 ranked matches.

## Identity

**Strengths:** DEF anchor. Rook (1,106r, 62.0% WR) is the dominant career identity — reliable anchor with consistent win rates across Mute, Mozzie, Kapkan, Kaid. DEF side is where TazRathmus earns rounds.

**Tendencies:** Hard-utility breach on ATK (Thermite/Sledge/Gridlock all high-volume), but with losing WRs across the board. The ATK side is functional, not a strength. DEF is clearly the more natural role.

**Role fit:** DEF anchor / support. Rook opener is ideal — sets up the team. Mute, Mozzie, Kapkan all show solid career WRs. Not a roamer; likely plays close and holds.

**Solo vs. Group:** Insufficient recent data to assess. Career volume (1,238 ranked) suggests experience, but recent inactivity (3 ranked Y10S4, 0 Y11S1) means current form is unknown.

## Career Coaching Notes

- **DEF anchor is the strongest contribution.** Rook/Mute/Mozzie/Kapkan/Kaid all above 54% WR with meaningful rounds — consistent, not a fluke.
- **ATK side loses rounds.** Thermite (753r, 41.4% WR), Gridlock (717r, 41.6%), Sledge (667r, 41.8%) — huge volume, below 50% WR across all three. ATK needs work or op pool needs rethinking.
- **Smoke (26r, 50.0% WR, 1.00 K/D)** is a potential upside op — better K/D than most DEF pool, has rounds. Could develop into a plant-denial closer.
- **Very low recent activity.** Peak was Deadly Omen season (298 matches, 2,886 RP). Recent seasons are near-absent. Treat current skill level as unknown until meaningful Y11S1 data exists.
